title: Phase 0: Pattern Extraction
description: Extract patterns from existing 49 convert-* skills to ground IR design in real conversion knowledge. Mine implicit IR patterns before designing the formal schema.
notes:
Deliverables:
- analysis/pattern-extraction.md
- data/patterns.sql
- data/pattern-clusters.json

Effort: 4-5 days
close_reason: Phase 0 complete. All 15 subtasks done. Extracted 7,195 patterns from 49 skills, clustered into 173 universal + 417 family + 3,293 language-specific. 320 gaps identified.

title: 0.0 Skill Structure Analysis
description:
Preliminary analysis before building extraction tooling.

1. Sample 5 representative skills manually:
   - convert-python-rust (Dynamic → Systems)
   - convert-python-haskell (Dynamic → ML-FP)
   - convert-c-cpp (Systems → Systems)
   - convert-erlang-elixir (BEAM → BEAM)
   - convert-java-kotlin (Managed → Managed)

2. Document observed sections:
   - Quick Reference tables
   - Type System Mapping (nested: Primitives, Collections, etc.)
   - 'This Skill Does NOT Cover' (negative patterns)
   - 'When Converting' guidelines
   - Error handling sections
   - Async/concurrency sections

3. Identify parent skill patterns:
   - Most skills extend meta-convert-dev
   - Extract parent patterns as part of each child skill
close_reason: Closed

title: 0.1 Pattern Extraction
description:
For each of the 49 convert-* skills, extract patterns including inherited content from meta-convert-dev:

Pattern types to extract:
- type_mappings: source → target type equivalences
- idiom_translations: code pattern translations
- semantic_gaps: concepts that can't be directly translated
- negative_patterns: anti-patterns (what NOT to do)
- guidelines: process guidance
- scope_boundaries: from 'Does NOT Cover' sections
- error_handling: exception/result patterns
- concurrency: threading/async model translations
- tool_recommendations: tooling suggestions

Bidirectional Handling: For skills marked bidirectional (29 of 49), extract patterns twice — once for each direction.
notes:
Phase A (Structural Extraction) complete:
- 7,195 patterns extracted from 49 skills
- 6,026 type mappings, 312 tools, 299 guidelines, 214 idioms
- 42 skills detected as bidirectional (91 unique directions)
- Output: patterns.json, patterns.db, patterns.sql
- Analysis: pattern-extraction.md created

Phase B (LLM-Assisted) deferred - structural extraction captured majority of structured content.
close_reason: Extracted 7,195 patterns from 49 skills. Phase A (structural extraction) complete. Deliverables: patterns.json, patterns.db, patterns.sql, pattern-extraction.md

title: 0.2 Pattern Clustering
description:
Analyze extracted patterns to identify clusters:

| Cluster | Definition | Example |
|---------|------------|---------|
| Universal | Applies to all/most conversions | Function signatures, basic types |
| Family-specific | Applies within a language family | Pattern matching in ML-FP family |
| Language-specific | Unique to a language pair | F# type providers → Roc |
| Inherited | From meta-convert-dev | APTV workflow, testing strategies |

Output: data/pattern-clusters.json
close_reason: Clustered 7,195 patterns into 3,883 unique groups: 173 universal (2,186 instances), 417 family-specific (1,332 instances), 3,293 language-specific (3,677 instances)

title: 0.3 Gap Analysis
description:
From extracted patterns, identify:

1. Lossy conversions - What information is lost?
2. Human decisions - What requires manual choice?
3. Impossible conversions - What cannot be translated?
4. Negative patterns - What should explicitly NOT be done?

Document findings in analysis/pattern-extraction.md
close_reason: Gap analysis complete: 320 gaps identified (29 negative, 183 human decisions, 108 lossy). Report: analysis/clustering-and-gaps.md

title: 0.4 Validation (Sampling)
description:
Validate extraction quality:

1. Randomly sample 10% of extracted patterns
2. Manual review for accuracy
3. Flag patterns with confidence: low for review
4. Document extraction failures or ambiguities

Acceptance criteria:
- 10% sample validated
- Extraction failures documented
close_reason: Validation complete: 719 patterns sampled (10%), 7 minor issues found (table markers), 99%+ patterns valid. Report: validation-report.md

title: analyze fit of spec-workflow-mcp
description:
https://github.com/Pimzino/spec-workflow-mcp
also 'claude-code-spec-workflow'

title: add frontmatter scopes
description: add scopes (user/project/component/etc) to each context file

title: add command namespacing in frontmatter
description:
use build time logic to remove illegal frontmatter keys

Organize commands in subdirectories. The subdirectories are used for organization and appear in the command description, but they do not affect the command name itself. The description will show whether the command comes from the project directory (`.claude/commands`) or the user-level directory (`~/.claude/commands`), along with the subdirectory name.

Conflicts between user and project level commands are not supported. Otherwise, multiple commands with the same base file name can coexist.

For example, a file at `.claude/commands/frontend/component.md` creates the command `/component` with description showing "(project:frontend)".
Meanwhile, a file at `~/.claude/commands/component.md` creates the command `/component` with description showing "(user)".

```yaml
---
allowed-tools: Bash(git add:*), Bash(git status:*), Bash(git commit:*)
argument-hint: [message]
description: Create a git commit
model: claude-3-5-haiku-20241022
---
```

title: question: is it a good pattern to use progressive disclosure in sub-agents?
Phase 4: IR Schema Design
description:
Design the multi-layer Intermediate Representation architecture that can represent code semantics from any supported language and enable high-fidelity conversion.

## Goal
Create comprehensive IR schema with 5-layer architecture (Layers 0-4), supporting partial extraction modes.

## Dependencies
- Phase 3: Semantic Gaps (completed)

## Key Deliverables
- IR layer documentation (overview + 5 layers)
- JSON Schema definitions (ir-v1.json)
- SQL schema extensions (ir-schema.sql)
- Binary format documentation (for future extractor CLI)
- Pattern validation (ir-validation.md)

## Success Criteria
- All 5 layers fully specified with YAML schemas
- JSON schemas validate against examples
- Can represent all 54 gap patterns from Phase 3
- 10 patterns validated against IR

title: 4.1 Architecture Overview
description:
Create IR architecture overview documentation.

## Deliverable
`docs/src/ir-schema/overview.md`

## Contents
- 5-layer architecture diagram
- Layer responsibilities
- Extraction modes (full_module, single_function, type_only, signature_only)
- Layer relationships and data flow
- Schema versioning approach

## Inputs
- Layer architecture diagram from §4.1 of plan
- Phase 3's ir-implications.md for annotation requirements
- ADR-008 for content hashing

## Acceptance
- Overview document covers all 5 layers
- Extraction modes clearly documented
- Cross-references Phase 3 gap patterns
close_reason: Created overview.md (365 lines) covering 5-layer architecture, extraction modes, annotations, gap markers, preservation levels, and content hashing.

title: 4.10 JSON Schema Definitions
description:
Create JSON Schema definitions for IR validation.

## Deliverable
`schemas/ir-v1.json`

## Contents
Define JSON Schema for all IR types:
- Layer 4: module, import, export, definition
- Layer 3: type_def, type_param, type_body, type_ref, type_relationship
- Layer 2: function, param, effect, control_flow_graph, block, terminator
- Layer 1: binding, lifetime, data_flow_node, transformation
- Layer 0: expression, source_span
- Cross-cutting: semantic_annotation, gap_marker, preservation_status, asymmetry_info

## Requirements
- Draft-07 or later JSON Schema
- Examples for each type
- Required vs optional fields clearly marked
- Enum constraints for kind fields

## Acceptance
- Schema validates against example IR documents
- All 5 layers have schemas
- Annotation kinds include all 54 Phase 3 patterns
close_reason: Created ir-v1.json (1705 lines) - complete JSON Schema draft-07 with all 5 layers, 54 annotation kinds, 6 gap types

title: 4.11 Pattern Validation
description:
Validate IR schema against Phase 0 patterns.

## Deliverable
`analysis/ir-validation.md`

## Process
1. Select 10 representative patterns from patterns.sql:
   - 3 type mappings (TS-001, TS-002, TS-003)
   - 3 memory patterns (MM-001, MM-002, MM-004)
   - 2 effect patterns (EF-001, EF-004)
   - 2 concurrency patterns (CC-001, CC-004)

2. For each pattern show:
   - Source code example
   - IR representation at each relevant layer
   - Annotations required
   - Gap markers if applicable

## Output Format
| Pattern | Source | IR Layers | Annotations | Gaps |
|---------|--------|-----------|-------------|------|
| TS-001  | Python | 1,2,3     | inferred_type | structural |

## Acceptance
- All 10 patterns have IR representations
- IR captures semantic intent, not just syntax
- Gap markers correctly reference Phase 3 IDs
close_reason: Created ir-validation.md (1120 lines) validating IR schema against 10 representative patterns: 3 type system (TS-001/002/003), 3 memory model (MM-001/002/004), 2 effect system (EF-001/004), 2 concurrency (CC-001/004). All patterns validated successfully.

title: 4.12 Final Review
description:
Final review of Phase 4 deliverables against quality gates.

## Quality Gates

| Gate | Validation |
|------|------------|
| Schema completeness | All layer schemas have required fields |
| Pattern coverage | All 54 gap patterns expressible in annotations |
| Preservation integration | All 4 levels trackable per IR unit |
| Decision point linkage | 16 decision points referenceable from gaps |
| SQL compatibility | Extensions work with existing schema.sql |

## Checklist
- [ ] All 5 layers fully specified with YAML schemas
- [ ] JSON schemas validate against examples
- [ ] Can represent all 54 gap patterns from Phase 3
- [ ] Partial extraction modes documented
- [ ] Gap markers integrated with Phase 3 pattern IDs
- [ ] Preservation level tracking in schema
- [ ] SQL extensions build on existing schema.sql
- [ ] 10 patterns validated against IR

## Deliverable
`analysis/phase4-review.md`

## Acceptance
- All quality gates pass
- No blocking issues remaining
- Phase 4 ready for Phase 5 consumption

title: 4.2 Layer 4: Structural IR
description:
Document Layer 4 (Structural IR) schema.

## Deliverable
`docs/src/ir-schema/layer-4.md`

## Schema Elements
- module: id, name, path, visibility, imports, exports, definitions
- import: id, module_path, imported_items, alias
- export: id, item, alias, visibility
- definition: kind (type|function|constant|variable), ref
- metadata: source_file, source_language, extraction_version, extraction_mode

## Key Features
- Represents high-level code organization
- Module/package/namespace boundaries
- Public/private visibility
- Import/export dependencies
- Build/compilation units

## Acceptance
- All schema elements documented with examples
- Partial extraction support (extraction_scope: full|partial)
close_reason: Created layer-4.md (783 lines) - Structural IR with module, import, export schemas and Python/Rust/Java examples

title: 4.3 Layer 3: Type IR
description:
Document Layer 3 (Type IR) schema.

## Deliverable
`docs/src/ir-schema/layer-3.md`

## Schema Elements
- type_def: id, name, kind, params, constraints, body, visibility
- type_param: name, variance, bounds, default
- type_body: fields, methods (struct/class), variants (enum), required/provided_methods (interface)
- type_ref: kind (named|generic|function|tuple|union|intersection), args
- type_relationship: from_type, to_type, kind (extends|implements|subtype_of|convertible_to)

## Key Features
- Type definitions (ADTs, classes, interfaces)
- Type relationships (subtyping, implements, extends)
- Generic/parametric types
- Type constraints and bounds
- Variance annotations

## Acceptance
- All schema elements documented with examples
- Type relationships include variance handling
- Generic type parameters fully specified
close_reason: Created layer-3.md (1043 lines) - Type IR with 7 type kinds, variance, HKT handling, and 4 language examples

title: 4.4 Layer 2: Control Flow IR
description:
Document Layer 2 (Control Flow IR) schema.

## Deliverable
`docs/src/ir-schema/layer-2.md`

## Schema Elements
- function: id, name, params, return_type, type_params, effects, body, visibility, receiver
- param: name, type, default, mutability
- effect: kind (pure|throws|async|unsafe|io|suspends|allocates|captures), type
- control_flow_graph: entry, blocks, exit
- block: id, statements, terminator
- terminator: kind (return|branch|switch|loop|try), associated data

## Key Features
- Function/method signatures
- Control patterns (branch, loop, match, try)
- Effect annotations (pure, throws, async)
- Concurrency patterns (spawn, await, channel)
- Control flow graph representation

## Acceptance
- All terminators documented with examples
- Effect system fully specified
- Concurrency patterns from Phase 3 CC-* patterns supported
close_reason: Created layer-2.md (1303 lines) - Control Flow IR with 8 effect kinds, CFG, terminators, and concurrency patterns

title: 4.5 Layer 1: Data Flow IR
description:
Document Layer 1 (Data Flow IR) schema.

## Deliverable
`docs/src/ir-schema/layer-1.md`

## Schema Elements
- binding: id, name, type, mutability, lifetime, value, scope
- lifetime: kind (static|scoped|owned|borrowed), scope, borrow_kind
- data_flow_node: id, kind (source|transform|sink), expression, inputs, outputs, effects
- transformation: kind (map|filter|fold|flat_map|collect), input_type, output_type, function

## Key Features
- Variable bindings and lifetimes
- Data transformations (map, filter, fold)
- Side effect tracking
- Dependency graph between expressions
- Ownership/borrowing model support

## Acceptance
- Lifetime system documented with examples
- Supports MM-* memory patterns from Phase 3
- Mutability tracking (mutable|immutable|linear)
close_reason: Created layer-1.md (926 lines) - Data Flow IR with ownership/borrowing, lifetimes, linear types, and closure captures

title: 4.6 Layer 0: Expression IR
description:
Document Layer 0 (Expression IR) schema.

## Deliverable
`docs/src/ir-schema/layer-0.md`

## Schema Elements
- expression: id, kind, type, span
  - literal: literal_kind (int|float|string|bool|null), literal_value
  - identifier: binding_ref
  - call: callee, arguments
  - operator: operator, operands
  - lambda: params, body, captures
- source_span: file, start_line, start_col, end_line, end_col

## Key Features
- Full AST representation
- Operator semantics
- Literal values and constants
- Source location tracking

## Note
Layer 0 is **optional** - most conversions use Layers 1-4 only.
Use when: preserving formatting, complex expression transforms, debugging, round-trip testing.

## Acceptance
- Schema clearly marked as optional
- Use cases documented
- Source span tracking for debugging
close_reason: Created layer-0.md (809 lines) - Expression IR (optional) with all expression kinds and guidance on when to use

title: 4.7 Cross-Cutting Concerns
description:
Document cross-cutting IR concerns: annotations, gap markers, preservation tracking.

## Contents (in layer docs or overview)

### Semantic Annotations
Integrate 54 gap patterns from Phase 3:
- Type System (16): inferred_type, nullability, hkt_specialization, type_erasure_info, variance_annotation
- Memory Model (12): ownership_hint, ownership_transfer, mutability_analysis, lifetime_hint, arc_cycle_risk
- Effect System (12): error_handling_conversion, null_to_option, monad_flattening, evaluation_strategy
- Concurrency (14): concurrency_model_conversion, channel_to_async, supervision_conversion, thread_safety

### Gap Markers
- gap_type: impossible|lossy|structural|idiomatic|runtime|semantic
- gap_pattern_id: Reference to TS-*, MM-*, EF-*, CC-* patterns
- severity, mitigations, decision_point_id

### Preservation Level Tracking
- current_level: 0-3
- max_achievable_level: 0-3
- blocking_gaps, level_evidence

### Asymmetry Metadata
- source_family, target_family
- direction_difficulty, reverse_difficulty
- asymmetry_ratio (e.g., 4.0 for GC→Ownership)

## Acceptance
- All 54 Phase 3 patterns expressible as annotations
- Preservation levels (0-3) trackable per IR unit
- 16 decision points referenceable from gaps
close_reason: Created cross-cutting.md (723 lines) - all 54 annotation kinds, gap markers, preservation tracking, asymmetry metadata, and 16 decision points

title: 4.8 Binary Format Documentation
description:
Document binary formats for future universal extractor CLI.

## Deliverable
`docs/src/ir-schema/binary-formats.md`

## Contents
1. **Protobuf schema specification** - .proto file structure matching YAML/JSON schemas
2. **Usage guidelines** - When to use Protobuf vs JSON (file size, streaming)
3. **MessagePack considerations** - Embedded scenarios where Protobuf overhead excessive
4. **Cross-language compatibility** - Notes for Python, Rust, Go, TypeScript consumers
5. **Schema evolution rules** - Adding fields without breaking consumers

## Scope
**Documentation only** - not implemented in this project.
A future 'universal extractor' CLI will consume these specs.

## Why document now?
- Ensure JSON schema and future Protobuf schema stay aligned
- Provide architectural guidance for future implementers
- Identify JSON constructs that don't map cleanly to Protobuf

## Acceptance
- Proto message definitions for all IR types
- Clear guidance on JSON↔Protobuf mapping
- Schema evolution strategy documented
close_reason: Created binary-formats.md (1214 lines) - Protobuf schema specs, MessagePack considerations, cross-language compatibility

title: 4.9 SQL Schema Extensions
description:
Create SQL schema extensions for Phase 4 IR tables.

## Deliverable
`data/ir-schema.sql`

## New Tables (extends schema.sql)
1. **ir_preservation_status** - Preservation level tracking
   - unit_id, current_level, max_achievable_level
   - blocking_gaps (JSON), level_0/1/2/3_achieved

2. **ir_asymmetry_metadata** - Bidirectional conversion info
   - unit_id, source_family, target_family
   - direction_difficulty, reverse_difficulty, asymmetry_ratio

3. **ir_decision_resolutions** - Human decision logging
   - unit_id, decision_point_id, chosen_option
   - rationale, resolved_by, resolved_at

## ALTER TABLE extensions
- ir_gaps: Add gap_pattern_id, severity, target_concept, decision_point_id, preservation_level

## Indexes
- idx_preservation_unit, idx_preservation_level
- idx_asymmetry_unit, idx_asymmetry_families
- idx_decisions_unit, idx_decisions_point

## Acceptance
- SQL syntax valid (runs after schema.sql)
- All new tables have proper foreign keys
- Indexes created for common query patterns
close_reason: Created ir-schema.sql (233 lines) - preservation_status, asymmetry_metadata, decision_resolutions tables with indexes, views, triggers

title: Phase 2: Language Survey
description: Catalog languages with family classification and detailed feature profiles. Create comprehensive profiles for all languages in the conversion ecosystem, enabling accurate IR extraction and synthesis. 22 full profiles (Tier 1-2) + 7 minimal profiles (Tier 3). Effort: 10-14 days.

title: 2.0 Extract Languages from Phase 0
description: Extract authoritative language list from Phase 0 patterns.db using SQL queries. Query source/target languages from skill names. Output: Verified language list for Tier 1. Effort: 0.5 days.

title: 2.4 Tier 1 Profiles - Managed-OOP Family
description: Create full profile for java (+ version history 8, 11, 17, 21). Document records, pattern matching, sealed classes evolution. Effort: ~0.5 days.

title: 2.4 Tier 1 Profiles - Apple Family

title: 2.5 Tier 2 Profiles

title: 2.6 Tier 3 Minimal Profiles

title: 2.7 Coverage Gap Analysis
description: Analyze convert-* skill coverage. Query patterns.db for skill names, parse source/target languages, cross-reference with gap-analysis.json. Build coverage matrix showing As Source/As Target/Bidirectional/Missing pairs. Output: analysis/coverage-gaps.md. Effort: 1 day.

title: 2.8 SQL Schema Extension
description: Extend Phase 1 SQL schema with: language_versions (for major languages), language_ecosystem (tooling), language_popularity (snapshots), language_relationships (influenced_by, compiles_to, ffi). Populate with profile data. Output: data/languages.sql. Effort: 1 day.

title: 2.9 Review & Cross-Reference
description: Final review: verify all profiles cross-reference Phase 1 family taxonomy, validate quality gates (completeness, family alignment, source attribution, cross-references, SQL consistency). Effort: 1 day.

title: 2.1 Language Prioritization
description: Finalize tier assignments: Tier 1 (18 languages from convert-* skills), Tier 2 (4 high-value: kotlin, gleam, zig, ruby), Tier 3 (7 minimal: cobol, fortran, pascal, ada, lisp, scheme, prolog). JavaScript and TypeScript are separate profiles.
close_reason: Created data/language-tiers.yaml with finalized tier assignments: 17 Tier 1, 5 Tier 2, 7 Tier 3. Includes priority rankings, rationale, and version history flags for major languages.

title: 2.2 Version Policy
description: Document version policy for profiles. Major languages (Python, Java, C, C++) include historical versions with breaking changes. Other Tier 1/2: latest stable only. Tier 3: representative version.

title: 2.3 Data Sources
description: Establish authoritative data sources for each profile field: type system (official spec), memory model (spec/runtime docs), syntax (grammar/BNF), popularity (TIOBE/StackOverflow), ecosystem (package registries). Define freshness policy for metrics.
close_reason: Created data/data-sources.yaml with official sources for all 29 languages, metrics sources (TIOBE, SO Survey, GitHub), and freshness policy for popularity snapshots.

title: 2.4 Tier 1 Profiles - Dynamic Family
description: Create full profiles for Dynamic family languages: python (+ version history 2.7, 3.6, 3.10), javascript, typescript. Use full template with ecosystem, syntax patterns, semantic gaps. Effort: ~1 day.

title: 2.4 Tier 1 Profiles - ML-FP Family
description: Create full profiles for ML-FP family languages: haskell, elm, roc, fsharp, scala. Use full template with ecosystem, syntax patterns, semantic gaps. Effort: ~1.5 days.

title: Phase 3: Semantic Gap Analysis
description: Identify and classify conversion challenges between language families. Create comprehensive catalog of semantic gaps that inform IR design.

title: 3.1 Classify Existing Phase 0 Gaps
description: Reclassify 320 gaps from Phase 0 into 6-category system: impossible, lossy, structural, idiomatic, runtime, semantic. Map 183 human_decision gaps to categories with requires_human_decision flag.

title: 3.10 SQL Data Population
description: Generate data/gaps.sql with INSERT statements for semantic_gaps, gap_patterns, decision_points, family_conversion_difficulty tables.

title: 3.11 Final Review & Deliverables
description: Compile analysis/semantic-gaps.md with all findings. Verify quality gates: classification complete, patterns have examples, matrix justified, SQL valid.

title: 3.2 Family Pair Analysis
description: Document gaps for 72 directed family pairs (9×8). For each pair: impossible, lossy, structural, runtime gaps with concepts, mitigations, and IR impact.

title: 3.3 Gap Severity Matrix
description: Create 9×9 matrix of conversion difficulty between all families. Populate with evidence-backed difficulty scores (1-4 scale).

title: 3.4 Bidirectional Gap Analysis
description: Identify asymmetric gaps where A→B ≠ B→A. Document: GC↔Ownership, Static↔Dynamic, Actors↔Threads, HKT↔No-HKT asymmetries.

title: 3.5 Common Gap Patterns
description: Document 50+ recurring gap patterns with from/to examples across: type system, memory model, effect system, concurrency categories.

title: 3.6 Semantic Preservation Levels
description: Define 4-level preservation model: Level 0 (syntactic), Level 1 (semantic), Level 2 (idiomatic), Level 3 (optimized). Output: docs/src/ir-schema/preservation-levels.md

title: 3.7 Human Decision Points
description: Catalog decision points from 183 human_decision gaps: error handling, null handling, concurrency model, memory management, generic instantiation, collection choice.

title: 3.8 Cross-Validate with Phase 2
description: Verify gaps align with semantic_gaps from 29 YAML profiles. Target: 90%+ mapping coverage. Document any language-specific gaps not in family analysis.

title: 3.9 IR Design Implications
description: Document how each gap category affects IR layer design. Map categories to layers 0-3 with design considerations.

description: Create matrices for cross-family analysis using graduated scale (none|limited|partial|full|native). Includes Feature×Family matrix and Conversion Difficulty matrix (7×7 families). Output: docs/src/language-families/overview.md
close_reason: Created docs/src/language-families/overview.md with Feature×Family matrices (5 categories: typing, memory, concurrency, meta, effects) and 7×7 Conversion Difficulty matrix with Phase 0 gap counts.

title: 1.4 Family Documentation
description: Create documentation for all 13 families following progressive disclosure template: Overview, Subtypes, Key Characteristics, Languages, Type System, Memory Model, Concurrency Model, Common Patterns, Conversion Considerations, Cross-References, Sources. Output: docs/src/language-families/{family}.md (13 files)
close_reason: Created 13 family docs: 7 paradigm (ml-fp, dynamic, beam, lisp, systems, managed-oop, apple), 4 feature (gradual-typing, ownership, actors, dependent-types), 2 specialized (logic, array). All follow progressive disclosure template.

title: 1.5 Cross-Reference with Phase 0
description: Validate family characteristics against Phase 0 extracted patterns. For each pattern cluster: identify family characteristics, note mismatches. For each family: list validating patterns, identify gaps. Adjust definitions based on observations. Output: Section in analysis/family-taxonomy.md
close_reason: Created analysis/phase0-crossref.md with full cross-reference analysis. Validated all 7 families against Phase 0 patterns. Updated family-taxonomy.md with results.

title: 1.6 Validation Sampling
description: Verify family documentation accuracy against representative skills. For each high-priority family (top 6): Select 2-3 convert-* skills, review against documentation, verify type system/memory model/difficulty alignment, document discrepancies. Output: Validation Matrix in analysis/family-taxonomy.md
close_reason: Validated all 6 high-priority families against 12 representative skills. Created analysis/validation-sampling.md. All families passed: type systems, memory models, and difficulty ratings align with skill content.
